overview.title: Electronic Components Library
overview.description: Essential passive and active electronic components including resistors, capacitors, transistors, ICs, and diodes with specifications, applications, and circuit examples

components.name: Carbon Film Resistor (1/4W)
components.shortDesc: Standard fixed resistor, 1/4 watt power rating, ±5% tolerance for general-purpose circuits
components.fullDescription: Carbon film resistors are the most common type of fixed resistor used in electronic circuits. They provide reliable resistance values at low cost and are suitable for most general-purpose applications where high precision is not critical.

identification.Reading: Band 1&2 (or 1,2,3): Digits | Band 3 (or 4): Multiplier | Last: Tolerance

codeSnippet.title: LED Current Limiting Calculation
codeSnippet.code:
// LED Current Limiting Resistor Calculation
// Ohm's Law: R = V / I

Given:
- Supply Voltage (Vs) = 5V
- LED Forward Voltage (Vf) = 2V (typical for red LED)
- LED Forward Current (If) = 20mA = 0.02A

Calculation:
Voltage across resistor = Vs - Vf = 5V - 2V = 3V
Resistance = V / I = 3V / 0.02A = 150Ω

Choose nearest standard value: 150Ω or 220Ω (safer)

Power dissipation:
P = V² / R = (3V)² / 220Ω = 0.041W
1/4W resistor is adequate (rated for 0.25W)

components.safetyNotes: Ensure power rating is not exceeded. Resistors can get hot when operating near their power limit.

components.name: 10kΩ Linear Potentiometer
components.shortDesc: Variable resistor with 10kΩ maximum resistance, linear taper for volume controls and voltage adjustment
components.fullDescription: A potentiometer is a three-terminal variable resistor that allows manual adjustment of resistance. The 10kΩ value is extremely common in electronics for volume controls, brightness adjustment, and as adjustable voltage dividers in sensor circuits.
useCases: User input controls in Arduino projects

codeSnippet.title: Arduino Potentiometer Reading

wiring.Terminal 1: Connect to VCC or one end of voltage divider
wiring.Terminal 3: Connect to GND or other end of divider

components.name: Ceramic Capacitor 100nF (0.1µF)
components.shortDesc: Non-polarized ceramic capacitor, 100nF value for decoupling, filtering, and timing applications
components.fullDescription: The 100nF (0.1µF) ceramic capacitor is one of the most essential components in electronics. Used primarily for decoupling power supplies and filtering high-frequency noise, it's found in virtually every digital circuit board.

identification.Code Format: First 2 digits + number of zeros in pF

codeSnippet.title: Decoupling Capacitor Placement
codeSnippet.code:
// Power Supply Decoupling Best Practices

Placement Rules:
1. Place 100nF ceramic capacitor as close as possible to each IC's VCC pin
2. Keep leads short to minimize inductance
3. Connect capacitor between VCC and GND pins
4. Use one 100nF capacitor per IC

Typical Circuit:
    VCC ────┬─── IC VCC Pin
            │
           [100nF]
            │
    GND ────┴─── IC GND Pin

Why 100nF?
- Effective at filtering high-frequency noise (>1MHz)
- Low ESR and ESL
- Inexpensive and widely available
- Standard value in digital designs

components.safetyNotes: Non-polarized - can be connected either way. Ensure voltage rating exceeds circuit voltage.

components.name: Electrolytic Capacitor 100µF/25V
components.shortDesc: Polarized aluminum electrolytic capacitor for power supply filtering and energy storage applications
components.fullDescription: Electrolytic capacitors offer high capacitance values in relatively small packages, making them ideal for power supply filtering, energy storage, and audio coupling. The 100µF/25V variant is commonly used in low-voltage DC power supplies.

identification.Polarity: Negative lead is shorter, case has stripe indicating negative side
identification.Warning: MUST be connected with correct polarity!

useCases: Timing circuits with long time constants

codeSnippet.title: Power Supply Filter Calculation
codeSnippet.code:
// Basic Power Supply Filter Design

Given:
- Unregulated DC input: 12V
- Load current: 500mA
- Ripple frequency: 100Hz (full-wave rectified 50Hz AC)
- Desired ripple voltage: < 0.5V peak-to-peak

Capacitance Calculation:
C = I / (f × ΔV)
where:
  I = load current (A)
  f = ripple frequency (Hz)
  ΔV = allowable ripple voltage (V)

C = 0.5A / (100Hz × 0.5V)
C = 0.5 / 50 = 0.01F = 10,000µF

Practical Design:
- Use 10,000µF or higher
- Voltage rating: Minimum 1.5× input voltage = 18V
- Choose 25V rated capacitor for safety margin
- Add 100nF ceramic in parallel for high-frequency filtering

components.safetyNotes: CRITICAL: Reverse polarity will cause explosion! Always verify polarity before powering up. Can store charge - discharge before handling.

components.name: 2N2222 NPN Transistor
components.shortDesc: General-purpose NPN bipolar junction transistor for switching and amplification up to 600mA
components.fullDescription: The 2N2222 is one of the most popular and widely-used NPN transistors in electronics. It's excellent for low-power switching applications, driving relays, LEDs, small motors, and general-purpose amplification circuits.

pinout.Identification: Flat side facing you, left to right: E-B-C

useCases: Driving relays and motors (with flyback diode)

codeSnippet.title: Arduino Relay Driver Circuit

components.safetyNotes: Always use current-limiting resistor on base. Add flyback diode when switching inductive loads (relays, motors).

components.name: IRF540N N-Channel MOSFET
components.shortDesc: Power MOSFET for high-current switching up to 33A, ideal for motor control and high-power loads
components.fullDescription: The IRF540N is a high-current N-channel power MOSFET designed for switching applications. With its low on-resistance and ability to handle up to 33A, it's perfect for controlling motors, high-power LEDs, and other demanding loads from microcontroller outputs.

pinout.Tab: Connected to Drain, can be heatsink mounted

codeSnippet.title: Arduino PWM Motor Control

components.safetyNotes: Use heatsink for high-current applications. Always add flyback diode for inductive loads. Gate-source voltage should not exceed 20V.

components.name: NE555 Timer IC
components.shortDesc: Versatile timer IC for oscillators, pulse generation, and timing applications from microseconds to hours
components.fullDescription: The NE555 is one of the most popular and versatile integrated circuits ever made. It can operate in three modes: astable (oscillator), monostable (one-shot), and bistable (flip-flop), making it invaluable for timing, pulse generation, and oscillator circuits.

pinout.Pin 4: RESET - Reset (active low, tie to Vcc if not used)
pinout.Pin 5: CTRL - Control voltage (optional, usually 10nF to GND)

codeSnippet.title: Astable Mode LED Blinker
codeSnippet.code:
// 555 Timer Astable (Oscillator) Configuration
// Creates square wave output to blink LED

Circuit:
  Vcc (Pin 8) ── +9V
  
  Pin 7 (DISCH) ──┬── R1 (10kΩ) ── Vcc
                  │
  Pin 6 (THRES) ──┴── R2 (10kΩ) ──┬── Pin 2 (TRIG)
                                   │
                             C1 (100µF) ── GND
  
  Pin 3 (OUT) ──[330Ω]── LED ──── GND
  
  Pin 4 (RESET) ── Vcc
  Pin 5 (CTRL) ──[10nF]── GND
  Pin 1 (GND) ── GND

Formulas:
  Frequency (f) = 1.44 / ((R1 + 2×R2) × C1)
  Duty Cycle = (R1 + R2) / (R1 + 2×R2)

Calculation:
  f = 1.44 / ((10k + 2×10k) × 100µF)
  f = 1.44 / (30k × 100µF)
  f = 1.44 / 3 = 0.48 Hz
  
  Period = 1/f = 2.08 seconds
  LED blinks approximately every 2 seconds

Adjustments:
- Increase C1 for slower blinking
- Decrease C1 for faster blinking
- Change R1/R2 ratio to adjust duty cycle

components.safetyNotes: Pin 4 (RESET) must be tied to Vcc if not used. Output can source/sink 200mA but use current-limiting resistor for LEDs.

components.name: LM7805 Voltage Regulator
components.shortDesc: Fixed +5V linear voltage regulator, 1.5A output for powering digital circuits and microcontrollers
components.fullDescription: The LM7805 is a three-terminal positive voltage regulator that provides a constant +5V output from a higher input voltage. It's the most common voltage regulator for powering 5V digital logic, microcontrollers, and other electronics requiring stable 5V DC supply.

pinout.Tab: Connected to middle pin (GND), heatsink mountable

codeSnippet.title: Basic 5V Power Supply Circuit
codeSnippet.code:
// LM7805 Basic Voltage Regulator Circuit
// Input: 9-12V DC (from battery or wall adapter)
// Output: Stable 5V @ up to 1.5A

Circuit:
  INPUT (9-12V) ──┬── C1 (100nF ceramic) ── GND
                  │
                  └── Pin 1 (INPUT)
                  
  Pin 2 (GND) ── GND
  
  Pin 3 (OUTPUT) ──┬── C2 (100µF electrolytic +) ── GND
                   │
                   ├── C3 (100nF ceramic) ── GND
                   │
                   └── +5V OUTPUT to load

Component Selection:
- C1 (100nF ceramic): Input filter, reduces input noise
- C2 (100µF/16V electrolytic): Output smoothing, improves transient response
- C3 (100nF ceramic): High-frequency filtering

Heatsink Requirements:
Power dissipation = (Vin - 5V) × I_load
Example: 12V input, 500mA load
Pd = (12V - 5V) × 0.5A = 3.5W

Thermal calculation:
Tjunction = Tambient + (Pd × Rθ)
For TO-220 without heatsink: Rθ ≈ 50°C/W
Tj = 25°C + (3.5W × 50°C/W) = 200°C (OVERHEATING!)

Solution: ADD HEATSINK to reduce Rθ
With small heatsink: Rθ ≈ 15°C/W
Tj = 25°C + (3.5W × 15°C/W) = 77.5°C (OK)

Rule of Thumb:
- For Pd > 1W, use heatsink
- Keep Vin as low as possible (7-9V ideal for 5V output)
- Use switching regulator for higher efficiency

components.safetyNotes: Can get very hot! Use heatsink for currents >500mA. Input capacitor required for stability. Ensure adequate ventilation.

components.name: LM358 Dual Op-Amp
components.shortDesc: Dual operational amplifier IC for signal amplification, filtering, and analog signal processing
components.fullDescription: The LM358 is a dual operational amplifier (op-amp) IC containing two independent, high-gain amplifiers in a single package. It's designed for single supply operation and is ideal for sensor signal conditioning, audio amplification, and general analog circuit applications.

pinout.Pin 2: IN- A - Op-amp A inverting input
pinout.Pin 3: IN+ A - Op-amp A non-inverting input
pinout.Pin 5: IN+ B - Op-amp B non-inverting input
pinout.Pin 6: IN- B - Op-amp B inverting input

codeSnippet.title: Non-Inverting Amplifier Configuration
codeSnippet.code:
// LM358 Non-Inverting Amplifier Circuit
// Amplifies input signal by gain factor

Circuit (using Op-Amp A):

  Input Signal ── Pin 3 (IN+ A)
  
  Pin 2 (IN- A) ──┬── R1 (10kΩ) ── GND
                  │
                  └── R2 (10kΩ) ── Pin 1 (OUT A) ── Output
  
  Pin 4 (GND) ── GND
  Pin 8 (Vcc) ── +9V
  
  Bypass: 100nF capacitor between Pin 8 and Pin 4

Gain Calculation:
  Voltage Gain (Av) = 1 + (R2 / R1)
  
  With R1 = R2 = 10kΩ:
  Av = 1 + (10k / 10k) = 2
  
  Output = Input × 2

Example Values:
- Input: 1V → Output: 2V
- Input: 2.5V → Output: 5V

For different gains:
- Gain = 2: R2 = R1
- Gain = 5: R2 = 4 × R1 (e.g., R1=10k, R2=40k)
- Gain = 10: R2 = 9 × R1 (e.g., R1=10k, R2=90k)
- Gain = 11: R2 = 10 × R1 (e.g., R1=10k, R2=100k)

Design Tips:
1. Keep R1 between 1kΩ - 100kΩ
2. Don't exceed supply voltage at output
3. Add 100nF bypass capacitor near Vcc pin
4. Use voltage divider if input can exceed Vcc

components.safetyNotes: Output cannot swing to full supply rails. Keep signals within supply voltage range. Add bypass capacitor for stability.

components.name: 74HC595 8-Bit Shift Register
components.shortDesc: Serial-in parallel-out shift register for expanding GPIO pins and driving multiple LEDs/displays
components.fullDescription: The 74HC595 is an 8-bit serial-in, parallel-out shift register with output latches. It allows you to control 8 outputs using only 3 microcontroller pins, making it perfect for LED matrices, 7-segment displays, and GPIO expansion.

pinout.Pin 10: MR - Master reset (active low, tie HIGH)
pinout.Pin 13: OE - Output enable (active low, tie LOW)

codeSnippet.title: Arduino 8-LED Control with 74HC595

components.safetyNotes: Use current-limiting resistors with LEDs. Total output current should not exceed 70mA. Pin 10 (MR) must be HIGH for normal operation.

components.name: L293D Motor Driver IC
components.shortDesc: Dual H-bridge motor driver for controlling two DC motors or one stepper motor bidirectionally
components.fullDescription: The L293D is a popular dual H-bridge motor driver IC that can control two DC motors independently or one stepper motor. It features built-in flyback diodes and can handle up to 600mA per channel, making it ideal for small robot and hobby projects.

pinout.Pin 1, 9: EN1, EN2 - Enable pins (PWM for speed control)
pinout.Pin 2, 7: IN1, IN2 - Motor A direction control
pinout.Pin 10, 15: IN3, IN4 - Motor B direction control

codeSnippet.title: Arduino Dual Motor Control

components.safetyNotes: Can get hot with high loads - use heatsink if needed. Separate motor power supply recommended for motors >100mA. Connect all ground pins.

components.name: Standard 5mm LED
components.shortDesc: Common 5mm light-emitting diode in various colors for indicators, displays, and lighting projects
components.fullDescription: The 5mm LED is the standard building block for electronic lighting and indicators. Available in multiple colors (red, green, blue, yellow, white), these LEDs are simple to use and extremely reliable for hobbyist and professional projects alike.

specifications.Colors Available: Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, White, RGB

identification.Polarity: Longer lead = Anode (+), Shorter lead = Cathode (-)
identification.Flat Edge: Flat side of LED indicates cathode side

codeSnippet.title: Arduino LED Blink with Proper Resistor

components.safetyNotes: ALWAYS use current-limiting resistor! Reverse polarity can damage LED. Exceeding maximum current will reduce lifespan or destroy LED.

components.name: 1N4007 Rectifier Diode
components.shortDesc: General-purpose rectifier diode, 1A/1000V for AC-DC conversion and flyback protection circuits
components.fullDescription: The 1N4007 is the most common general-purpose rectifier diode in electronics. With its 1000V reverse voltage rating and 1A forward current capability, it's perfect for AC-DC power supplies, flyback protection for inductive loads, and reverse polarity protection.

identification.Band: Band on diode body marks cathode (negative) end
identification.Arrow Symbol: Arrow points from anode to cathode
identification.Current Flow: Conducts when anode is positive relative to cathode

useCases: Full-wave bridge rectifiers in power supplies
Flyback/freewheeling diodes for relays and motors

codeSnippet.title: Flyback Diode Protection Circuit
codeSnippet.code:
// Flyback (Freewheeling) Diode for Relay/Motor Protection
// Protects switching transistor from voltage spikes

Why Needed:
When current through an inductor (relay coil, motor) is suddenly 
interrupted, it generates a high-voltage spike (back-EMF) that can 
destroy transistors and ICs.

Circuit:
                    +12V
                     │
                   [Relay]
                   [Coil ]
                     │
        ┌────────────┴────────┐
        │                     │
       ═══ 1N4007           [C] Transistor collector
      (Flyback)              │
      Diode                  │
        │                   [E] Emitter
        │                     │
       GND ──────────────────┴──── GND

Connection:
- Cathode (banded end) → +12V or relay coil positive
- Anode → Ground side of relay coil
- Diode is reverse-biased during normal operation
- When coil turns OFF, diode provides path for collapsing magnetic field

Without Diode:
- Voltage spike can reach 100V+
- Transistor base-emitter junction destroyed
- MCU pins damaged

With Diode:
- Spike clamped to ~0.7V above supply
- Components protected
- Slight delay in relay release (energy dissipation)

Always use with:
- Relays, solenoids, motors
- Any inductive load switched by transistors

components.safetyNotes: Observe correct polarity. Can get warm in high-current applications. Not suitable for high-frequency switching (use Schottky for that).

components.name: BMP280 Pressure & Temperature Sensor
components.shortDesc: High-precision barometric pressure and temperature sensor for altitude and weather monitoring
components.fullDescription: The BMP280 is a digital barometric pressure and temperature sensor from Bosch Sensortec. It provides high accuracy pressure measurements (±1 hPa) and temperature readings, making it ideal for altitude measurement, weather stations, and indoor navigation systems.

codeSnippet.title: Arduino BMP280 Reading

components.safetyNotes: Operates at 3.3V logic. Use level shifter if connecting to 5V microcontrollers without built-in protection.

components.name: MPU6050 6-Axis IMU Sensor
components.shortDesc: 6-axis motion tracking with 3-axis gyroscope and 3-axis accelerometer for robotics and motion sensing
components.fullDescription: The MPU6050 is a popular 6-axis motion tracking device combining a 3-axis gyroscope, 3-axis accelerometer, and temperature sensor. With built-in Digital Motion Processor (DMP), it provides accurate motion and orientation data, perfect for robotics, drones, and VR/AR applications.

codeSnippet.title: MPU6050 Basic Reading

components.safetyNotes: Ensure proper I2C pull-up resistors (typically 4.7kOhm). Module usually includes these.

components.name: RC522 RFID Module
components.shortDesc: 13.56MHz RFID reader/writer module for access control and contactless identification
components.fullDescription: The RC522 is a highly integrated RFID reader/writer module operating at 13.56MHz. It supports ISO/IEC 14443 A/MIFARE cards and provides SPI, I2C, and UART interfaces. Ideal for access control systems, inventory management, and contactless payment applications.

wiring.ESP32: Use any GPIO pins for SPI

codeSnippet.title: RC522 Card Reading

components.safetyNotes: CRITICAL: Use only 3.3V power! 5V will damage the module. Many modules include level shifters for 5V MCU compatibility.

components.name: PN532 NFC/RFID Module
components.shortDesc: Advanced NFC module supporting reader/writer, P2P, and card emulation modes
components.fullDescription: The PN532 is a highly integrated NFC controller supporting multiple protocols including ISO14443A/B, FeliCa, and ISO/IEC 18092. It can operate in three modes: reader/writer, peer-to-peer, and card emulation, making it versatile for NFC payments, data sharing, and access control applications.

specifications.Supported Tags: MIFARE, NTAG, FeliCa, ISO14443 Type A/B

wiring.I2C (Default): SDA to A4, SCL to A5 (Arduino Uno)
wiring.Mode Selection: Usually via onboard switches or jumpers

codeSnippet.title: PN532 NFC Tag Reading

components.safetyNotes: Module typically includes voltage regulator for 5V operation. Check module specifications for power requirements.

components.name: DS18B20 Waterproof Temperature Sensor
components.shortDesc: OneWire digital temperature sensor with high accuracy, available in waterproof probe version
components.fullDescription: The DS18B20 is a digital temperature sensor using the 1-Wire protocol, allowing multiple sensors on a single data line. With ±0.5°C accuracy and available in waterproof stainless steel probe versions, it's ideal for applications requiring temperature monitoring in harsh or wet environments.

identification.OneWire Address: Each sensor has unique 64-bit ROM code

codeSnippet.title: Arduino DS18B20 Reading

components.safetyNotes: Requires 4.7kOhm pull-up resistor between data and VCC. Waterproof versions rated IP68.

components.name: NEO-6M GPS Module
components.shortDesc: Compact GPS module with high accuracy and fast satellite acquisition for location tracking
components.fullDescription: The NEO-6M is a popular GPS module from u-blox featuring excellent sensitivity and fast time-to-first-fix. It provides accurate position, velocity, and time data via UART interface, making it perfect for GPS trackers, navigation systems, and location-based IoT projects.

wiring.Arduino: VCC to 5V, GND to GND, TX to Pin 4, RX to Pin 3 (SoftwareSerial)
wiring.ESP32: VCC to 3.3V, TX to any RX pin, RX to any TX pin
wiring.Note: Some modules are 3.3V logic - use level shifter for 5V MCUs

codeSnippet.title: Arduino GPS Data Reading

components.safetyNotes: Requires clear view of sky for satellite acquisition. Indoor use may not work. Cold start can take up to 30 seconds.

components.name: 5V Single Channel Relay Module
components.shortDesc: Optocoupler-isolated relay for controlling high-power AC/DC devices with microcontrollers
components.fullDescription: The 5V relay module allows microcontrollers to safely control high-voltage and high-current devices. Featuring optocoupler isolation for protection, it can switch up to 250VAC/30VDC at 10A, making it essential for home automation and appliance control projects.

wiring.Arduino: VCC to 5V, GND to GND, IN to Digital Pin
wiring.Load Connection: AC/DC device between COM and NO (or NC)
wiring.Safety: Never work on live AC circuits without proper safety measures

codeSnippet.title: Arduino Relay Control

components.safetyNotes: WARNING: Can switch mains voltage (110V/220V AC). Risk of electric shock. Only qualified personnel should work with AC power. Always disconnect power before wiring.

components.name: DS3231 Real-Time Clock Module
components.shortDesc: High-precision RTC with temperature-compensated crystal and battery backup for timekeeping
components.fullDescription: The DS3231 is an extremely accurate I2C real-time clock with integrated temperature-compensated crystal oscillator (TCXO). With battery backup capability and ±2ppm accuracy, it maintains accurate time even when the main power is off, perfect for data logging and scheduling applications.

specifications.Timekeeping: Seconds, minutes, hours, day, date, month, year

wiring.Arduino Uno: SDA to A4, SCL to A5, VCC to 5V, GND to GND
wiring.Arduino Mega: SDA to Pin 20, SCL to Pin 21
wiring.ESP32: SDA to GPIO21, SCL to GPIO22 (or any I2C pins)

codeSnippet.title: Arduino RTC Time Reading

components.safetyNotes: Install CR2032 battery with correct polarity. Battery backup ensures time is maintained during power loss.

components.name: Capacitive Soil Moisture Sensor v1.2
components.shortDesc: Corrosion-resistant capacitive sensor for accurate soil moisture measurement in plant monitoring
components.fullDescription: The capacitive soil moisture sensor measures soil moisture levels through capacitance changes rather than resistance, making it highly resistant to corrosion. Unlike resistive sensors, it doesn't make direct electrical contact with soil, ensuring long-term reliability for automated plant watering systems.

identification.vs Resistive: No exposed metal probes - uses capacitive sensing

codeSnippet.title: Arduino Soil Moisture Reading

components.safetyNotes: Requires calibration for accurate readings. Insert vertically into soil to proper depth. Do not submerge electronics portion.
